PUBLICATION MAP
Publications are grouped by the questions and methods they address.

The papers trace a progression from exact well-hydraulic solutions to delayed-response interpretation, transformation uncertainty, and groundwater-influenced energy decisions. Student-linked entries are marked only when the generated publication record explicitly supports that connection.

REPRESENTATIVE PUBLICATION
A Lagging Model for Describing Drawdown Induced by a Constant-Rate Pumping in a Leaky Confined Aquifer

Ying-Fan Lin and Hund-Der Yeh · Water Resources Research · 2017

DOI 10.1002/2017WR021115
RESEARCH QUESTION
What changes when flux and gradient need not respond at the same instant?

Can a pumping-test model represent a delayed relation between radial water flux and the drawdown gradient while retaining finite-well effects?

PHYSICAL SYSTEM
A finite pumping well in a leaky confined aquifer

A leaky confined aquifer pumped at a constant rate through a finite-radius well, with wellbore storage and skin included at the pumping well.

MATHEMATICAL RELATION
Generalized radial law

𝑞
Flux lag
𝜏
𝑠
Drawdown-gradient lag

When the two lags are equal, their effect cancels in the constitutive relation: equal-lag cancellation. When both lags are zero, the relation reaches the classical Darcy limit.

RESULT
Early-time agreement improved in the field comparison

For the Rapid City field case, the lagging formulation improved the early-time fit across five observation wells relative to the classical response used for comparison.

VIDEO
Research summary

Scope: the video summarizes the paper's model and evidence boundary; it is not a site-design calculation.

EVIDENCE BOUNDARY

The field comparison uses drawdown observations from five monitoring wells in Rapid City. It supports improved early-time agreement for this test and model configuration; it does not establish one lag pair for all aquifers.

Estimated lags are conditioned on the test data, boundary assumptions, leakage representation, finite well radius, wellbore storage, skin, and the selected model. They are fitted model parameters, not universal material constants.

CITATION

Lin, Y.-F., & Yeh, H.-D. (2017). A lagging model for describing drawdown induced by a constant-rate pumping in a leaky confined aquifer. Water Resources Research, 53, 8500–8511.
